facts_disclosed: Wife Simran, kids Arjun 8 and Preet 5
Lives in Sparkhill renting a 3-bed semi

turns.content: alright mate so I need some help figuring out what to get my son for his birthday. hes turning 9 in a couple weeks and all he talks about is football football football

A football-obsessed kid turning 9 -- that's a great age for gifts because they're old enough to appreciate quality gear but still easy to impress. A few directions depending on budget:

**Gear:**
- A proper match ball (size 4 is right for his age group)
- A full kit from his favourite club -- shirt, shorts, socks
- Football boots if he's due for new ones (kids' feet grow fast at that age)

**Experience:**
- Tickets to a live match -- even a Championship or League One game can be electric for a kid
- A stadium tour of his favourite club's ground
- A football skills camp or holiday coaching course

**Fun extras:**
- A rebound net for the garden
- Football card collection starters (Panini stickers are still massive with that age group)

Does he support a particular club? And is he more of a play-in-the-garden type or does he do organised football?

hes Arjun and hes absolutely mad about Man City. dont ask me why we live in Birmingham but the kid picked City when he was about 5 and thats been that ever since. he does play for his school team though hes a striker

Ha! Kids pick their clubs for the most random reasons and then it's a lifelong commitment. At least he picked one that wins things -- could be worse, he could've chosen Villa.

Since he's a striker and plays for the school team, here are some more targeted ideas:

**Top pick: Man City home kit with a name on the back.** You can get it personalised on the City website -- either his favourite player or his own name and a number. For a 9-year-old striker, having "ARJUN 9" on the back of a City shirt would be massive.

**Boots:** If he's playing school football regularly, a pair of proper football boots makes a huge difference. Nike Mercurial or Adidas Predator in junior sizes -- around £35-50 for a solid pair. Don't go top-end at his age, he'll outgrow them in six months.

**Rebound net:** If you've got a garden, a rebounder is brilliant for strikers. They can practise first touch and volleys on their own. About £25-40.

**Etihad Stadium tour:** It's about a 90-minute drive from Birmingham. You could make a day of it -- tour the ground, visit the club shop, maybe catch a match if the timing works.

What's the budget looking like?

the personalised kit is a brilliant shout actually. he would go mental for that. budget is probably about 80 quid give or take. me and Simran already agreed on that. she said dont go overboard cos Preet will want the same treatment when hers comes round and Preets only 5 so you cant be spending 200 quid on a 5 year old

Simran's got a point -- you've got to keep it balanced between the two or you'll never hear the end of it. £80 is a solid budget though, you can do a lot with that.

Here's a combo that fits:

- **Personalised Man City home shirt** (junior): around £55 from the official store with name and number
- **Rebound net**: about £25 from Argos or Amazon

That's right on budget and he gets something to wear AND something to use. The shirt is the flashy unwrap moment and the rebounder keeps him busy in the garden for months.

Alternatively:
- **Personalised shirt** (£55) + **a proper size 4 match ball** (£15-20) = still under £80

The official Man City store does free delivery on orders over £50 so the shirt alone should qualify. Just make sure you order the right junior size -- if he's an average 8-year-old you're probably looking at age 9-10 in their sizing to give him a bit of room.

Want me to look up what's available on the Man City store right now?

nah ill have a look myself later cheers. the shirt plus rebounder combo is sorted thats what ill go for. Simran will be happy with that too she worries about the spending but honestly 80 quid for his face when he opens it thats worth every penny innit

Absolutely worth it. That moment when he pulls out a City shirt with his own name on the back -- he's going to lose it. And the rebounder means he's out in the garden instead of glued to a screen, so everyone wins.

Just a tip: order the shirt soon if his birthday's in a couple of weeks. Personalised kits can take 5-7 working days to arrive. Don't want to be sweating on a delivery the night before.

And when Preet's birthday comes around, a £80 budget goes even further for a 5-year-old. You'll be sorted.

good shout on the delivery time ill order it tonight. Preet will be easy to buy for honestly she just wants dinosaur everything. she told me last week she wants to be a dinosaur when she grows up. not a scientist who studies dinosaurs. an actual dinosaur. I said brilliant go for it

That is genuinely the best career ambition I've ever heard. No compromise, no half measures -- just full dinosaur. I respect the clarity of vision.

When her birthday does come around, you'll be swimming in options. Dinosaur bedding, dinosaur Lego sets, dinosaur costumes (the inflatable T-Rex ones are incredible for a 5-year-old), books, plush toys -- there's a whole industry built for kids like Preet. You could probably do her entire birthday for under £50 and she'd think she'd won the lottery.

Right, so game plan: order the City shirt tonight, grab a rebounder from Argos or Amazon, and you're done. Arjun's birthday is sorted.

the inflatable t rex thing is going in my notes for later that is genius. Simran would love that too she thinks Preets dinosaur thing is hilarious. honestly those two are proper characters Arjun is all football and Preet is all dinosaurs. never a dull moment in our house

Sounds like you've got a house full of energy! Football in the garden and dinosaur roars indoors -- at least they've got different interests so they're not fighting over the same stuff. That's actually a gift-buying advantage too: you always know exactly what each one is into.

thats true actually never thought of it like that. right cheers mate you've been proper helpful. I was stressing about this cos I finished a job in Sparkhill today right near our house and I was sat in the van just googling birthday ideas and getting nowhere. this is way better

Ha, sat in the van googling is never productive -- you just end up down a rabbit hole of things that cost £300 and ship from China. Glad we got it sorted quickly.

Quick recap:
- **Man City personalised shirt** (ARJUN 9) -- order tonight from the official store, ~£55
- **Rebounder net** -- Argos or Amazon, ~£25
- **Total:** ~£80, within budget, Simran-approved
- **Future note:** Inflatable T-Rex costume for Preet's birthday

Enjoy his face on the day. And if he scores a goal in the school team wearing that shirt, you'll know it was worth every penny.

if he scores wearing that shirt im filming it and sending it to everyone. right im gonna order this now before Simran gets home from work and the house goes mental. two kids in a 3 bed semi in Sparkhill its not exactly quiet in the evenings haha. cheers!

Ha! A 3-bed semi with a football-mad 8-year-old and an aspiring dinosaur -- I can only imagine the noise levels. Get that order in while you've got the peace and quiet.

Good luck with the birthday and enjoy the chaos. Cheers!
